From my origins to how China's move to coal-to-olefins pushed naphtha into mogas, to the Golden Era of Refining (or not)... Great stuff to share! 

YouTube link in comments.

"Major oil pipelines ramping up now to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. All in, when fully ramped up, it’s 52%-64% of Gulf oil exports that could circumvent the Strait." 

#OOTT
quoted_text:
Major pipelines ramping up now to bypass the Strait of Hormuz. All in, when fully ramped up, it’s 52%-64% of Gulf exports that could circumvent the Strait:

Saudi Arabia — East–West Pipeline, the “Petroline” fm Abqaiq (Persian Gulf side) to Yanbu (Red Sea).
Capacity up to 5 million barrels/day.

United Arab Emirates — Habshan–Fujairah pipeline “ADCOP,” fm Abu Dhabi inland oil fields to Fujairah, Gulf of Oman, outside Hormuz. 
Capacity: 1.5 million barrels/day.

Iraq — Kirkuk–Ceyhan pipeline
fm N
